codeReview.summary: This fix narrows ownership to VAL-CROSS-005 and adds a checked-in proof file, but it still does not clear the prior blocker. Commit `a039b57` only adds `.factory/validation/mission-verification/mission-cross-flow-kill-worker-pause-proof.json`; it does not add any new Mission-GUI-TEST runtime artifact or test. The new bundle re-labels pre-existing foundation/routing/runtime-control artifacts as feature-owned proof, yet the cited runtime-controls evidence still says destructive kill validation used isolated renderer-state injection, and the paused-chat evidence it reuses is a generic paused-session continuation check rather than a same-session user-kill completion path. As a result, the feature still lacks direct same-session Mission-GUI-TEST proof that a real kill-worker action produced `worker_failed(reason = "Killed by user") -> mission_paused` while leaving the same Mission inspectable and resumable.

issues.description: The new proof bundle overstates what the underlying runtime-controls artifact proves. It claims the same flow shows a paused chat screenshot after the user-kill path, but the cited source separates those facts: `runtime-controls-kill-pending.png` is a synthetic pre-async pending-kill check with no completing network call, while the paused-chat screenshot is used for a generic paused-session continuation assertion. See `runtime-controls.json:23`, `:28`, `:233`, and `:267`. This still does not provide a same-session Mission-GUI-TEST end-to-end kill-worker -> paused/resumable proof.

The fix remains a proof-ownership remap, not new runtime proof. Commit `a039b57` only adds this JSON bundle, and the bundle itself acknowledges that destructive kill execution in Mission-GUI-TEST was exercised via isolated in-memory renderer-state injection. The rest of the cited interactive evidence (`foundation-metadata.json` and `routing-toggle.json`) proves session identity and inspectability for session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391`, but not a real kill-worker transition on that session. Older artifacts therefore remain the sole substantive evidence for VAL-CROSS-005, so the prior scrutiny failure is not actually resolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `mission-verification-worker` now says proof-audit sessions must not close a feature unless they add owned proof, but this run still marked `followedProcedure: true` after only adding an ownership bundle that remaps older artifacts and no fresh Mission-GUI-TEST kill-worker evidence. The skill should explicitly distinguish 'new owner file that cites existing evidence' from 'new feature-owned proof that satisfies a same-session E2E requirement', so workers do not treat relabeling prior artifacts as sufficient closure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/skills/mission-verification-worker/SKILL.md:37-47 and :49-53 require full-flow evidence and say older artifacts cannot be the sole proof, while /Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/handoffs/2026-03-10T00-47-41-191Z__mission-cross-flow-kill-worker-pause-proof__da09f276-e0c4-42c4-a477-2563d04c6f53.json:10 and :76 claim the feature now owns same-session proof and followed procedure even though commit `a039b57` only adds a remapping file.

summary: Scrutiny failed. The new feature-owned VAL-CROSS-005 bundle is checked in, but it still reuses pre-existing artifacts and focused tests rather than adding direct same-session Mission-GUI-TEST evidence that a real kill-worker action on the cited Mission session produced the paused/resumable lifecycle. The original blocker therefore remains.
